status_legend.in_progress: The feature is the current active task.
status_legend.blocked: Work cannot continue until a documented blocker is resolved.
status_legend.passing: Required verification has passed and evidence is recorded.

features.title: Store only to R2 (remove local and S3 storage backends)
features.user_visible_behavior: All stored files (transcriptions, sentences, translations, subtitle tracks) are written to Cloudflare R2 only; the API never writes them to the local filesystem and no longer serves a local /files mount.
verification: grep src/ for LocalFileAdapter, S3Adapter, storage_backend, local_storage, StaticFiles — no matches.
tests/test_storage.py proves get_storage() returns R2Adapter and raises StorageError listing missing R2_* env vars.
evidence: 2026-06-10: ./init.sh — 5 tests passed, ruff all checks passed, app boots with 12 routes.
2026-06-10: grep for removed symbols across src/, tests/, scripts/, alembic/ returned no matches.
features.notes: R2 config is validated lazily in get_storage(), so the app still boots without R2_* env vars but any storage use fails with an explicit StorageError. Implemented on branch feature/r2-only-storage.

features.title: Store only compressed sentence JSON as {video_id}.json
features.user_visible_behavior: Transcribing a video stores exactly one object in R2: a gzip-compressed sentence JSON at transcriptions/{video_id}.json (Content-Type: application/json, Content-Encoding: gzip, so the public URL still serves plain JSON to HTTP clients). No raw transcription dump is stored; cached transcribe/sentences/translate responses are derived from the sentence payload.
verification: tests/test_sentence_storage.py proves: single object per transcription at transcriptions/{video_id}.json, bytes are gzip, decompressed payload contains only sentence JSON (no top-level text/words/segments), cached reads round-trip, translation reads/writes compressed sentence JSON.
evidence: 2026-06-10: uv run pytest — 10 passed; uv run ruff check src tests — all checks passed.
features.notes: gzip chosen over zstd/brotli: stdlib, and via HTTP Content-Encoding every browser/client decodes transparently while the key keeps the .json name. Speaker segments are no longer cached (cached responses return segments: []). Old *_sentences.json and raw {video_id}.json objects in the bucket are orphaned, not migrated. Implemented on branch feature/sentence-json-storage.
